summary: Implemented issue #243 by adding the second bounded local MCP tool, specbridge.next-task. The tool is listed by tools/list, can be called through tools/call, returns a text content item containing the full existing next-task selector snapshot, and keeps unknown tool and missing tool-name failures deterministic. Existing specbridge.operator.status and resource read behavior remain compatible. Claude Code was used for next-task planning and a bounded implementation audit; ChatGPT/Codex completed implementation and local validation inside the active contract.
validations: Claude Code bounded implementation audit: passed
GitHub Actions SpecBridge Review Gate: not_collected
GitHub Actions SpecBridge PR Review Report: not_collected
GitHub Actions Claude Review Non Blocking: not_collected
policy_result: Within policy. The new tool is local-only and read-only in effect, wraps existing next-task selector data, writes no files, performs no external calls, installs no dependencies, and does not access secrets, production, billing, authentication, authorization, database, CI/CD security, deployment, cleanup enforcement, branch cleanup, artifact retention enforcement, issue #194 lifecycle, network MCP transport, hosted MCP server, or GitHub/resource mutation through MCP.
risk_result: Low risk and contained. The change extends an existing local allowlist with one read-only tool and test coverage, with no new data sources or mutation authority.
unresolved_risks: GitHub Actions evidence is not collected until the PR is pushed.

Future mutation-capable MCP tools, network MCP transport, hosted MCP server deployment, GitHub/resource mutation, cleanup enforcement, and artifact retention enforcement remain blocked future work requiring dedicated contracts.
merge_status: Not merged yet. PR and GitHub Actions evidence are pending.
deployment_status: Not applicable. No deployment is allowed by this contract.
